keyPoints: Emphasize the diversity-sampling intervention (Arm B) with 3–5 diverse options and explicit contrastive explanations per task.
Ground the chapter in QUEST: adopt Planning, Implementation/Adjudication, and Scoring phases, with evaluator training and pre-registered hypotheses.
Operationalize originality, calibration, and factual accuracy with concrete metrics: diversity/novelty scores, reliability calibration metrics (ECE/MCE/Brier), and expert fact-check adjudication.
Implement concurrent A/B design in real-work tasks: random assignment, task-type blocking, and ecological-validity considerations across domains.
Prioritize adjudication reliability: multiple rounds, inter-rater reliability stats (kappa/ICC), blinded scoring, and reconciliation processes.
Integrate risk management and governance: monitor factual accuracy, include fact-check gates, provide explainability/opt-out, preregister protocol, and emphasize reproducibility.
